description: SettleMint - What We Built: Jan-Mar 2026 | Internal team review deck covering Product, Marketing, and Business.

slides.title: Product
slides.subtitle: Runtime architecture, token features, auth hardening, CLI/SDK, and observability

slides.title: DALP Runtime Split: DAPI / DDWF / DIDX
slides.col1:
**DAPI - Data API Layer**
• Exposes token ops and asset management API surface
• Auth control plane hardened: browser sessions, org-scoped API keys, passkeys
• Clean service boundary enables targeted scaling
slides.col2:
**DDWF - Durable Workflow Engine**
• Durable execution substrate for resumable long-running operations
• Orchestrates token lifecycle end-to-end without state loss on failure
• Grafana alerting and runtime telemetry integrated
slides.col3:
**DIDX - Standalone Indexer**
• Extracted as an independently deployable indexer service
• API observability log warehouse captures all platform API calls
• Decoupled from core runtime for independent release cadence

slides.title: Token Feature Expansion
slides.item1:
**Conversion**
Token-to-token conversion logic shipped. Supports configurable conversion ratios and on-chain execution with full compliance gate coverage before state change.
slides.item2:
**Voting**
On-chain voting module added to token configuration. Governance decisions can be enforced at the token layer with weighted vote counts and quorum controls.
slides.item3:
**Yield Distribution**
Yield accrual and distribution logic shipped. Interest-bearing tokens can compute and distribute yield to holders on a configurable schedule.
slides.item4:
**Maturity Date**
Maturity date enforcement added. Tokens can be configured with a hard redemption deadline enforced at the smart contract layer.
slides.item5:
**AUM Fee Engine**
Assets-under-management fee calculation and deduction logic shipped. Fee parameters are set per asset class and applied automatically at the defined cadence.
slides.item6:
**Transaction Fees**
Per-transaction fee rules added to the token configuration model. Fees are collected atomically at time of transfer and routed to the configured recipient.

slides.title: DALP CLI and SDK Released
slides.col1:
**DALP CLI - Experimental Release**
• Device auth flow for secure CLI-to-platform pairing
• Typed operations: asset deployment, token lifecycle, configuration
• Monitoring and event streaming from the terminal
• Built for CI/CD pipeline integration
• Programmatic control without portal interaction
slides.col2:
**DALP SDK - Experimental Release**
• Strongly typed SDK covering all DALP APIs and event subscriptions
• Custom connector development and embedding into existing infrastructure
• Asset issuance, compliance enforcement, settlement event hooks
• Ships alongside CLI as a complete developer toolset
• Compose platform functions without using the operator portal

slides.title: Observability and Platform Reliability
slides.item1:
**API Observability Log Warehouse**
All API calls across the platform are now captured in a structured log warehouse. Supports audit, debugging, usage analysis, and compliance evidence without manual log extraction.
slides.item2:
**Runtime Telemetry**
Structured telemetry pipeline ships event data from all runtime services to observability tooling. Latency, error rates, and throughput are visible in real time across DAPI, DDWF, and DIDX.
slides.item3:
**Grafana Alerting**
Grafana dashboards and alert rules deployed against runtime telemetry. Teams receive proactive notifications on threshold breaches before customers are impacted.
slides.item4:
**Durable Execution Substrate**
Long-running token operations run on a durable execution substrate that survives process restarts and infrastructure failures. Operations resume from last known state without manual intervention.

slides.title: Marketing
slides.subtitle: DALP narrative, launch packaging, competitor intelligence, and pipeline automation

slides.title: DALP Narrative and Launch Package
slides.col1:
**Composable Tokens + Compliance**
• DALP positioning refined around composable token architecture with built-in compliance modules
• Messaging sharpened to lead with outcomes: regulated token issuance without fragmented infrastructure
• Narrative tested against buyer language from active enterprise deals
• Positions SettleMint as the platform for compliant, configurable digital asset issuance at scale
slides.col2:
**CLI / SDK Launch Package**
• Dedicated launch package built for the DALP CLI and SDK experimental releases
• Covers developer-facing positioning, feature narrative, and channel copy for announcements
• Documentation, quickstart materials, and API reference aligned to the launch package framing
• Coordinated with product release timing to ensure consistent messaging across channels
slides.col3:
**Competitor Intelligence System**
• Competitor intelligence coverage built and structured for ongoing use
• Key platform competitors mapped on feature depth, compliance posture, and go-to-market positioning
• Intelligence feeds directly into sales enablement materials and positioning updates
• Designed as a living system: new entrants and feature shifts are tracked and integrated continuously

slides.title: Pipeline Intelligence and CRM Enrichment
slides.col1:
**HubSpot Enrichment and Target Accounts**
• HubSpot enriched with target account intelligence: company size, sector, decision-maker mapping, and deal-relevant context
• Target account list built from ICP criteria and validated against current pipeline
• Enrichment feeds are automated to keep data fresh without manual CRM hygiene work
• Sales team has structured account context at the point of outreach and proposal generation
slides.col2:
**MEDDPICC Automation and Release Scanner**
• MEDDPICC qualification criteria embedded into CRM workflows to enforce consistent deal scoring
• Release scanner cron monitors product and ecosystem releases, feeding the content pipeline automatically
• Marketing intelligence automation surfaces timely signals for positioning and content updates
• Removes manual monitoring as a bottleneck: the system reacts to releases within hours rather than days

slides.title: Business
slides.subtitle: Live pipeline, strategic partnerships, and pipeline discipline

slides.title: Live Pipeline Highlights
slides.body: Active opportunities across banking, infrastructure, insurance, and real estate tokenization

slides.title: Strategic Partnerships
slides.col1:
**aXedras - Precious Metals**
• Partnership confirmed for precious metals tokenization on DALP
• BIL engaged as issuing institution
• Asset parameters in active scoping
• First precious metals tokenization partnership in production scope
slides.col2:
**CMA / DEPO-X - Bond POC**
• SettleMint confirmed as the tokenization platform
• 835-line technical spec mapped to DALP capabilities
• Four-week POC timeline agreed
• Covers issuance, compliance, settlement, investor lifecycle
slides.col3:
**KSA Real Estate Registry**
• Strategic engagement for tokenized real estate infrastructure
• SettleMint as the core tokenization platform layer
• Regulatory alignment with KSA digital asset framework ongoing
• Flagship public-sector registry use case in the Gulf region

slides.title: Pipeline Discipline: MEDDPICC Standard
slides.item1:
**200+ Zombie Deals Removed**
Over 200 stale, unqualified deals cleared from the CRM. The pipeline is now an accurate picture of real activity - not a graveyard of dead leads inflating the numbers and obscuring forecasting.
slides.item2:
**15-20 Live Deals Preserved**
Fifteen to twenty qualified opportunities retained with clear owners, defined next steps, and realistic close dates. Each deal has a named contact, documented economic buyer, and a confirmed decision process.
slides.item3:
**MEDDPICC Adopted as Standard**
MEDDPICC qualification framework applied across all live deals. Distinguishes qualified opportunities from noise, gives leadership confidence in pipeline coverage, and sets a consistent bar for deal entry.
slides.item4:
**Board Reporting Cadence**
Regular board-level pipeline reporting established. Executive visibility is delivered on a consistent schedule, replacing ad hoc updates with structured review tied to the live deal set.

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Architecture and process diagrams for reference

slides.title: DALP Runtime Architecture Split
slides.body:
The DALP runtime was decomposed into three independently deployable services in Jan-Mar 2026.

• DAPI: API surface, auth control plane, and org-scoped key management
• DDWF: durable workflow engine with resumable long-running operations
• DIDX: standalone indexer with its own deployment lifecycle

The split enables targeted scaling, cleaner failure isolation, and independent release cadences for each service layer.

slides.title: Token Feature Expansion - Capability Map
slides.body: Six new token features shipped in Q1 2026: conversion, voting, yield, maturity, AUM fee, and transaction fees. All features are applied at the token configuration layer and execute through the same on-chain compliance gate.

slides.title: DALP CLI / SDK Developer Flow
slides.body: The CLI and SDK give developers a complete programmatic toolset for DALP without requiring portal interaction.

slides.title: Live Pipeline by Segment
slides.body:
The 15-20 active deals span six segments:

• Banking: OCBC, DBS, UOB
• Payments infrastructure: Swift DLT Layer
• Insurance: BNP Paribas Cardif
• Commodities: aXedras pilot
• Real estate: KSA Registry
• Regional cluster: Japan, KBC Solar

All deals MEDDPICC-qualified with named owners and agreed next actions.

slides.title: Pipeline Hygiene Timeline - Jan-Mar 2026
slides.body: Zombie deal removal, MEDDPICC rollout, and board reporting cadence established across January through March.
